colLabels: {@filter Maneuvers Known|maneuvers|Tradition=biting zephyr;mist and shade;rapid current}

classFeature.name: Skill Tricks
entries: Your focused training allows you to use your skills in ways that others can't. At 1st level you gain a skill trick of your choice. Your tricks are detailed in the {@filter Optional Features|optionalfeatures|Feature Type=ST} page. The Tricks Known column of the Rogue table shows when you learn more skill tricks. Unless otherwise noted, you can gain each trick only once.

classFeature.name: Sneak Attack
entries: Beginning at 1st level, you fight with precision, taking advantage of momentary lapses in an opponent's concentration. Your attacks with Dexterity-governed or ranged weapons can deal additional base damage. The amount of extra damage is shown in the Sneak Attack column of the Rogue table.

In order to apply this damage, you must not have disadvantage on the attack and your target must be adjacent to another enemy of the target who is not {@condition incapacitated}. Alternatively, you can apply sneak attack damage if you have advantage on the attack.

classFeature.name: Combat Maneuvers
entries: At 2nd level, you gain the ability to use combat maneuvers. You gain proficiency in two combat traditions from the following list: {@filter Biting Zephyr, Mist and Shade, or Rapid Current|maneuvers|Tradition=biting zephyr;mist and shade;rapid current}. You learn two maneuvers of your choice from traditions you are proficient with.

You gain an exertion pool equal to twice your proficiency bonus, regaining any spent exertion at the end of a short or long rest. You use your maneuvers by spending points from your exertion pool. The Maneuvers Known column of the Rogue table shows when you learn more maneuvers from a tradition you are proficient with, while the Maneuver Degree column shows the highest degree you can select maneuvers from at a given level.
Additionally, whenever you complete a long rest, you can choose one of the maneuvers you know and replace it with another maneuver of the same degree from a tradition you are proficient with.

classFeature.name: Combat Tactic
entries: At 2nd level, you adopt a set of moves and tactics which complement your preferred combat strategies. Choose one of the following options. Whenever you complete a long rest, you may change this choice.

entries.name: Ambusher
entries: When you begin your turn hidden from a creature, you gain advantage on melee attacks against it until the end of your turn.

entries.name: Carver
entries: When you deal sneak attack damage with a knife weapon, your attack deals an additional {@dice 1d6} damage. At 11th level, this additional damage becomes {@dice 2d6} damage.

entries.name: Sniper
entries: Once per turn, when you make an attack with a ranged weapon you can choose to add your proficiency bonus to the damage roll.

classFeature.name: Cunning Action
entries: Also at 2nd level, you use your mobility to weave through the battlefield. Each turn in combat, you can use a bonus action to take the Dash, Disengage, Hide, or Use an Object action.

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 1
entries: At 1st level, you gain the tier 1 feature from your first subclass.

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 2
entries: At 3rd level, you gain the tier 2 feature from your first subclass.

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 3
entries: At 5th level, you gain the tier 3 feature from your first subclass.

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 4
entries: At 7th level, you gain the tier 4 feature from your first subclass.

classFeature.name: First Subclass Tier 5
entries: At 9th level, you gain the tier 5 feature from your first subclass.

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 1
entries: At 11th level, you gain the tier 1 feature from your second subclass.

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 2
entries: At 13th level, you gain the tier 2 feature from your second subclass.

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 3
entries: At 15th level, you gain the tier 3 feature from your second subclass.

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 4
entries: At 17th level, you gain the tier 4 feature from your second subclass.

classFeature.name: Second Subclass Tier 5
entries: At 19th level, you gain the tier 5 feature from your second subclass.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 4th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 8th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 12th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 16th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 19th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

classFeature.name: Innocent Facade
entries: Beginning at 4th level, you are skilled at hiding your motives. Choose one of the following options. You must be proficient in a skill to gain an expertise die from Innocent Facade.

entries.name: Cipher
entries: You gain an expertise die on Deception checks. You can invent ciphers, and are able to teach a cipher to others. Anyone who knows a cipher can encode and read hidden messages made with it; the apparent text must be at least four times longer than the hidden message. Other creatures can spend a minute to make an Investigation check (DC 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Intelligence modifier) to detect the presence of a cipher, and are able to read the hidden message if they substantially succeed.
Additionally, you have advantage on Investigation checks made to detect and decode ciphers and codes.

entries.name: Distraction
entries: You gain an expertise die on Persuasion checks. You can use an action to make a Persuasion check, opposed by the Insight check of creatures of your choice that can sense you. On a failure, until the end of your next turn a creature has disadvantage on Perception checks until it can no longer sense you.

entries.name: Social Expertise
entries: You gain an expertise die on checks made with either Finesse or Insight.

entries.name: Veiled Threat
entries: You gain an expertise die on Intimidation checks. When you succeed on a verbal Intimidation check, creatures of your choice other than the target of your check don't recognize your hostile intent.

classFeature.name: Aim
entries: Beginning at 5th level, you learn to briefly focus and make the most out of your next strike. If you haven't yet moved on your turn, you can use your Cunning Action to Aim, gaining advantage on the next armour attack roll you make before the start of your next turn. If you do so, your Speed is reduced to 0 until the end of your turn.

classFeature.name: Self-Preservation
entries: At 6th level, your honed reflexes and mental toughness allow you to avoid danger. Choose two of the following options. You may choose an additional option at 12th level and two more at 18th level.

entries.name: Blindsense
entries: You can use Cunning Action to reach out with your senses. Until the end of your next turn, you gain 10 feet of blindsight, so long as you are able to hear.

entries.name: Evasion
entries: Reflex attacks made against you are reduced by 1 success tier.

entries.name: Slippery Mind
entries: You gain +2 will.

entries.name: Street Tough
entries: You gain +2 fortitude.

entries.name: Tumbling Retreat
entries: When a damaging melee armour attack fails to hit you, you can use your reaction to shift up to half your speed.

entries.name: Uncanny Dodge
entries: When an attacker that you can see makes an armour attack against you, you can use your reaction to reduce the attack's success tier by 1.

classFeature.name: Improved Critical
entries: At 7th level, your weapon armour attacks have +1 critical success range.

classFeature.name: Shrewd Judgment
entries: Beginning at 8th level, your ability to read others makes you as dangerous in conversation as you are in a dark alley. Choose one of the following options. You must be proficient in a skill in order to gain an expertise die from Shrewd Judgment.

entries.name: Quick Frisk
entries: You gain an expertise die on Finesse checks. You can touch a target to learn what they are carrying; make a Finesse check opposed by the target's Perception. On a success, you learn what types of objects they are carrying. If they are carrying coins, you know the approximate number. For the next 24 hours, any Finesse checks you make against the target have advantage. On a failure, you can't use Quick Frisk against that target for 24 hours.

entries.name: Social Expertise
entries: You gain an expertise die on checks made with either Intimidation or Persuasion.

entries.name: Spot Tell
entries: You gain an expertise die on Insight checks. Whenever a creature makes a Deception check opposed by your Insight check and you win the opposed check, you also learn to recognize signs that the target is bluffing. For the next 24 hours, you have advantage on Insight checks made against the target.

entries.name: Tricky Interrogation
entries: You gain an expertise die on Deception checks. When you ask a yes or no question to a creature you can see, you can make a Deception check opposed by the target's Deception check. On a success, you learn whether the target believes the answer to be yes or no, even if they didn't reply. On a failure, you can't use Tricky Interrogation against that target for 24 hours.

classFeature.name: Low Profile
entries: Beginning at 10th level, you can avoid notice when you wish. Choose one of the following options. You must be proficient in a skill in order to gain an expertise die from Low Profile.

entries.name: Costumer
entries: You gain an expertise die on Deception checks made when using a disguise kit. When you use a disguise kit on yourself or another person, creatures have disadvantage on checks made to see through the disguise.

entries.name: Social Expertise
entries: You gain an expertise die on checks made with one of the following skills: Finesse, Insight, or Intimidation.

entries.name: True Lie
entries: You gain an expertise die on Persuasion checks. Your lies are so convincing that you half believe them yourself. You can use Persuasion instead of Deception when you are telling a lie.

entries.name: Walk it Back
entries: You gain an expertise die on Deception checks. After your party makes a group Deception check to tell a lie, you can use your reaction to add a supporting detail. Make an individual Deception check and replace the party's check with your own.

classFeature.name: Cunning Celerity
entries: Beginning at 11th level, you can take two different bonus actions on your turn. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus. You regain all uses of this feature when you complete a short or long rest.

classFeature.name: Expert Talent
entries: Beginning at 11th level, you almost never make mistakes when performing routine tasks. Whenever you make an ability check on which you roll expertise dice, you treat a {@dice d20} roll of 9 or lower as a 10.

classFeature.name: Self-Preservation (2)
entries: Also at 12th level, you can choose another option from your Self-Preservation feature.

classFeature.name: Guarded Aspect
entries: Beginning at 14th level, you are adept at uncovering and preserving secrets. Choose one of the following options. You must be proficient in a skill in order to gain an expertise die from Guarded Aspect.

entries.name: Hidden Signs
entries: You gain an expertise die on Finesse checks. You can use body language and hidden hand signals to communicate a short message to some observers while hiding it from others. Make a Finesse check opposed by the Insight checks of observers from whom you want to hide your communication. On a success, a creature is not aware that you sent a message. On a failure, the creature is aware that you signaled but doesn't know the content of your message. If you substantially fail, the creature learns your message.

entries.name: Master of Disguise
entries: You gain an expertise die on Deception checks made when using a disguise kit. As long as you are wearing a disguise, your true identity can't be determined even if your disguise fails. Additionally, you can don or doff a disguise as an action.

entries.name: Seen it All
entries: You gain an expertise die on Insight checks.

entries.name: Social Expertise
entries: You gain an expertise die on checks made with one of the following skills: Deception, Intimidation, or Persuasion.

classFeature.name: Elusive
entries: At 15th level, you know how to avoid vulnerable positions. As long as you are not {@condition incapacitated}, no armour attack roll has advantage against you.

classFeature.name: Overpowering Personality
entries: Beginning at 18th level, your poise and charm allow you to impose your vision on the world. Choose one of the following options. You must be proficient in a skill in order to gain an expertise die from Overpowering Personality.

entries.name: Dress Rehearsal
entries: You gain an expertise die on Persuasion checks. You can spend 10 minutes practicing a skill of your choice, at the end of which you make a Persuasion check. You can use this result to replace the die roll of one ability check you make using that skill in the next hour. You may decide to use this feature after seeing the die roll. Once you have used this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

entries.name: Psych Out
entries: You gain an expertise die on Intimidation checks. As an action, you can make an Intimidation check opposed by the Charisma check of a creature that can see you. On a success, the next armour attack roll or opposed ability check you make against that creature within the next 10 minutes is made with advantage. Once you've failed an opposed Intimidation check, you can't use this feature again until you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Roguish Charm
entries: You gain an expertise die on Persuasion checks. After you spend a minute in conversation with a creature that is not hostile, you may make a Persuasion check opposed by its Insight. On a success, the creature is nonmagically {@condition charmed} by you for 1 hour. The charm ends early if you or your allies hurt the creature. Once you've used this feature successfully, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Social Expertise
entries: You gain an expertise die on checks made with one of the following skills: Deception, Finesse, or Insight.

classFeature.name: Self-Preservation (4)
entries: Also at 18th level, you can choose another two options from your Self-Preservation feature.

classFeature.name: Grand Larcenist
entries: At 20th level, you reach the epitome of roguish talent and expertise. You gain the following abilities.

entries.name: Hide in Plain Sight
entries: You master the art of distraction. When a creature within your line of sight looks in your direction, you can use your reaction to hide even if you have nothing to hide behind as long as you remain in the same space without moving or taking actions. Until you move or take an action or a reaction, you are invisible to that creature. If you are targeted by a reflex attack, you may choose to increase its success tier by 1 to remain invisible to the creature, otherwise it reveals you.

entries.name: Skill Mastery
entries: You are unparalleled in your chosen fields. For each skill in which you have an expertise die, you gain another expertise die in that skill.

entries.name: Unstoppable
entries: No portal - be it door, window, or planar device - is closed to you. You no longer need to make ability checks to pick locks, and you succeed automatically whenever you attempt to unlock any lock of any kind.

optionalfeature.name: Agile Athlete
entries: You gain an expertise die on Athletics checks and a climb speed equal to your Speed.

optionalfeature.name: Boobytrapper
entries: You gain an expertise die on Finesse checks. You may make a Finesse check to conceal caltrops, ball bearings, a hunting trap, an alarm bell, or other Tiny traps or alarms. The result of your Finesse check is the DC for discovering these obstacles. They can be spotted without a check if they are not obscured.

optionalfeature.name: Cat's Eyes
entries: You gain an expertise die on Perception checks and 30 feet of darkvision.

optionalfeature.name: Delay Trap
entries: You gain an expertise die on checks made to pick locks or disarm traps. When you would trigger a mechanical trap on your turn, your alert senses warn you of danger before you complete the action. You may immediately end your turn in order to delay the trap's effects until the start of your next turn.

optionalfeature.name: Expertise Training
entries: You gain an expertise die on checks made with one of the following skills: Athletics, Finesse, Investigation, Perception, or Stealth. You may choose this trick more than once.

optionalfeature.name: Extra Skill Training
entries: You gain proficiency in one of the following: Athletics, Finesse, Investigation, Perception, or Stealth. You may choose this trick more than once.

optionalfeature.name: Graceful Leap

prerequisite.other: {@dice d8} expertise die on Finesse checks

entries: You gain an expertise die on Finesse checks. As a bonus action, you can jump up to half your Speed horizontally and up to 10 feet vertically. Opportunity attacks provoked by this movement are made with disadvantage.

optionalfeature.name: Hide in the Shadows
entries: You gain an expertise die on Stealth checks. You can attempt to hide from creatures without darkvision while you are in an area of dim light. When a creature tries to enter your space, your position is revealed.

optionalfeature.name: Keen Eye

prerequisite.other: {@dice d8} expertise die on Perception checks

entries: You gain an expertise die on Perception checks. You don't have disadvantage on Perception checks from being in lightly-obscured areas. If you have darkvision, you treat darkness and dim light as if it were bright light.

optionalfeature.name: Kip Up
entries: You gain an expertise die on Finesse checks. When you are {@condition prone}, standing up uses none of your movement. When you are knocked {@condition prone}, you can use your reaction to stand up.

optionalfeature.name: Locksmith
entries: You gain an expertise die on checks made to pick locks or disarm traps. You may spend a minute to improve an unlocked lock. At the end of the minute, make a Finesse check against the lock's DC. On a success, you permanently increase the lock's DC by 10. A lock may only be improved in this way once.
Additionally, you may spend a minute probing an unlocked lock. At the end of the minute, make a Magiscience check against the lock's DC. On a success, you make a key for the lock.

optionalfeature.name: Loot Runner
entries: You gain an expertise die on Athletics checks and your Speed increases by 5 feet. Additionally, you gain advantage on Treasure Hunt journey activity checks.

optionalfeature.name: Practiced Dash

prerequisite.other: {@dice d8} expertise die on Athletics checks

entries: You gain an expertise die on Athletics checks. When using the Dash action, you ignore difficult terrain and don't fall on slippery surfaces.

optionalfeature.name: Quick Scan
entries: You gain an expertise die on Investigation checks. You can use a bonus action to take the Search action.

optionalfeature.name: Scout Leader
entries: You gain an expertise die on Stealth checks. When your party makes a group Stealth check, you may apply your result to yourself and one ally after seeing the results of all rolls. Additionally, you gain advantage on Cover Tracks journey activity checks.

optionalfeature.name: Sense Aura

prerequisite.other: {@dice d8} expertise die on Investigation checks

entries: You gain an expertise die on Investigation checks. Additionally, you can spend 10 minutes to sense whether an object or creature bears magic. This feature is nonmagical.

optionalfeature.name: Sense for Secrets
entries: You gain an expertise die on Investigation checks. You have a sense for finding hidden things. You can spend a minute observing an area. If there are tricks, traps, secret doors, or other unusual features of a nonmagical nature within 30 feet of you on an unobscured surface you can see, you know something nearby is worth investigating, but not what or where it is. You can't use Sense for Secrets twice on the same area.

optionalfeature.name: Shadow Skulk

prerequisite.other: {@dice d8} expertise die on Stealth checks

entries: You gain an expertise die on Stealth checks. You have a passive Stealth score of 10 plus your Dexterity modifier plus your proficiency bonus. When you have not attempted to hide from a creature but are not visible to it and not making noise, you are considered hidden, using your passive Stealth score.

optionalfeature.name: Skeleton Key

prerequisite.other: {@dice d8} expertise die on Finesse checks

entries: You gain an expertise die on Finesse checks. As part of successfully picking a lock, you can alter the lock so that it opens to a key in your possession in addition to, or instead of, whatever keys previously opened it.

optionalfeature.name: Tuck and Roll
entries: You gain an expertise die on Finesse checks. When you would take falling damage, make a Finesse check and reduce the damage by an amount equal to the result of the check.
